data.body:
Correction / clarification (2026-04-27): the prior structural-sentinel coordination comment from 2026-04-26 referenced c183-ed2a as a single epic. On 2026-04-27 c183-ed2a was split into two epics on scrutiny review. Coordination surface is split across both halves:

- c183-ed2a (Epic A — inference channel): brainstorm Phase 1 Gate gains a completeness attestation field (SC1) and dso:red-team-reviewer gains a typed-envelope inference-challenge mode (SC2). The structural sentinel concept proposed in this epic's earlier comment applies to Phase 1 Gate's exit attestation: a sentinel artifact that the gate reads to verify the completeness attestation was actually emitted by an authorized step (not orchestrator-self-reported).

- a154-98de (Epic B — degradation channel): SC2 modifies brainstorm/phases/approval-gate.md to insert PRECONDITIONS degradation-emission bash blocks at the existing graceful-degradation prose paths. The structural sentinel concept proposed in this epic's earlier comment applies to approval-gate.md as well: the SCRUTINY: attestation line added to approval-gate.md on 2026-04-26 by the brainstorm session that surfaced this scope is the human-readable surface; this epic's structural sentinel SC would mechanically verify the attestation values against authentic sub-agent dispatches.

Both epics are now relates_to linked to this epic. When this epic implements the structural sentinel, the design must consider both attestation surfaces (Phase 1 Gate from Epic A, approval-gate.md from Epic B) in a single coordinated mechanism. When this epic and Epic B both modify approval-gate.md, the later-landing epic rebases; the sentinel insertion (this epic) and the degradation-marker insertion (Epic B) occupy non-overlapping textual regions.
